metadata.dispatch_line: Domestic disturbance reported at 1457 Oakwood Drive, apartment 3B, in the city of Riverton, Arizona, with a female yelling for help

scenario.dispatch_line: Domestic disturbance reported at 1457 Oakwood Drive, apartment 3B, in the city of Riverton, Arizona, with a female yelling for help

facts.domestic_violence_04: Yes, the officer provided the victim with resources and support
facts.domestic_violence_05: Priority response assigned through a 911 call

facts.domestic_violence_12: Yes, there is a valid protection order in place, issued on February 10, 2023
facts.domestic_violence_13: Yes, the scene is concisely described and diagramed in the report
facts.domestic_violence_14: Yes, photos were taken and details recorded, including visible injuries and property damage
facts.domestic_violence_15: The parties are identified as intimate partners, specifically boyfriend and girlfriend, living together at 1457 Oakwood Drive, Apartment 3B, Riverton, Arizona
facts.domestic_violence_16: The history of the relationship includes frequent verbal arguments and two previous incidents of physical violence, with the victim, Maya Ramos, reporting being intimidated and threatened by the suspect, Carlos Hernandez
facts.domestic_violence_17: Yes, all witnesses, including a neighbor, were interviewed and documented
facts.domestic_violence_18: Yes, an arrest was made, and the suspect, Carlos Hernandez, was taken into custody
facts.domestic_violence_19: Yes, there were two children, ages 7 and 9, on the scene, and they were removed to a safe location
facts.domestic_violence_20: Yes, information about previous incidents was documented, including police reports from January 2022 and June 2022
facts.domestic_violence_21: Yes, a kitchen knife was used as a weapon, and the suspect also used a chair to break the front door
facts.domestic_violence_22: The victim, Maya Ramos, was visibly shaken, crying, and reported feeling fearful and anxious
facts.domestic_violence_23: The suspect, Carlos Hernandez, was agitated and defensive, with a raised voice and clenched fists, during the initial interaction with the officer
facts.domestic_violence_24: Evidence collected includes photos of the damaged front door, a kitchen knife, and a broken chair, as well as witness statements from Maya Ramos and a neighbor, Ms. Leila Patel
facts.domestic_violence_25: Yes, a threat/risk assessment tool was used with the suspect, Carlos Hernandez, which indicated a high risk of future violence
facts.domestic_violence_26: Yes, evidence of fear is articulated in the report, including the victim's, Maya Ramos', statement that she felt fearful and anxious during the incident
facts.domestic_violence_27: Yes, all threats have been clearly documented, including the suspect's, Carlos Hernandez', threat to harm the victim if she did not drop the charges
facts.domestic_violence_28: Not applicable, as the suspect was present on the scene
facts.domestic_violence_29: Yes, the use of coercion and/or force is articulated in the report, including the suspect's use of a chair to break the front door and his attempt to intimidate the victim
facts.domestic_violence_30: Yes, all injuries, both visible and non-visible, have been documented, including the victim's bruised arm and neck pain
facts.domestic_violence_31: The injuries were new, sustained during the incident, and were documented in the report
facts.domestic_violence_32: Yes, there was property damage, including a broken front door and a damaged kitchen cabinet, with an estimated value of $500
facts.domestic_violence_33: Yes, stalking behaviors were identified, including the suspect's, Carlos Hernandez', repeated phone calls and texts to the victim, Maya Ramos, despite the protection order
facts.domestic_violence_34: Yes, the officer inquired about possible strangulation, and the victim reported experiencing difficulty breathing and neck pain during the incident
facts.domestic_violence_35: Yes, the victim, Maya Ramos, reported being strangled by the suspect, Carlos Hernandez, during a previous incident in June 2022
facts.domestic_violence_36: Yes, the victim, Maya Ramos, reported needing medical attention for her injuries and was transported to Riverton Medical Center by ambulance
facts.domestic_violence_37: No, the victim did not report sexual violence during the incident
facts.domestic_violence_38: Yes, all spontaneous statements were captured, including the suspect's, Carlos Hernandez', statement that he 'just lost control'
facts.domestic_violence_39: Yes, the officer asked about firearms, and the victim, Maya Ramos, reported that the suspect, Carlos Hernandez, has access to a handgun at his friend's house, 2345 Maple Street, Apartment 1, Aurora, CO 80010
facts.domestic_violence_40: No, the suspect did not use firearms during the incident
facts.domestic_violence_41: Yes, the suspect, Carlos Hernandez, made threats to use firearms against the victim, Maya Ramos, during a previous incident
facts.domestic_violence_42: Yes, the suspect, Carlos Hernandez, has access to firearms, according to the victim's statement
facts.domestic_violence_43: Yes, the victim, Maya Ramos, expressed fear about the suspect's, Carlos Hernandez', access to firearms and his potential to use them against her
facts.domestic_violence_44: No firearms were confiscated at the scene, but the officer noted the location of the suspect's firearm at 2345 Maple Street, Apartment 1, Aurora, CO 80010, and a search warrant is pending to confiscate the firearm

interview_transcript.content: Hello, did you really yell for help? Did something happen? Some series of unfortunate events?

(voice shaking) Oh, yes... I yelled for help. I was so scared. It was my boyfriend, Carlos. We were arguing, and it got out of control. He was yelling at me, and then he started breaking things. He broke the front door and came at me with a kitchen knife. I was trying to protect myself, but he was too strong. I thought he was going to hurt me really badly. That's when I yelled for help. I was so afraid. (starts crying)
Carlos, can I have his last name, last known address, mother's maiden name and social security number?

(sniffling) His last name is Hernandez. We live together at 1457 Oakwood Drive, Apartment 3B, in Riverton, Arizona. I don't know his mother's maiden name... I'm sorry, I don't remember him ever telling me. And I don't know his social security number either. I've never seen his social security card or anything. (shaking her head) I'm sorry, I don't know that information.
Is he a police officer? Did you know police officers commit a disproportionate amount of domestic violence?

(shaking her head) No, Carlos is not a police officer. He works in construction. I don't know anything about police officers and domestic violence... I just know that Carlos has a temper and can get really angry and scary when we argue. (pausing, looking down) I've been trying to leave him, but it's hard. We have two kids together, and I don't know how to make him leave me alone. (starting to cry again)

(tearfully) I'm just really scared of him. He's been getting worse and worse, and I don't know what he's capable of. He's been threatening me, saying he'll hurt me if I leave him or if I don't do what he wants. (shuddering) And tonight, when he broke the door and came at me with that knife... I thought it was all over. I thought he was going to kill me. (sobbing)
